content: Step-wise GRPO, a group-based reinforcement learning method that iteratively optimizes aligned step outputs, can be repurposed to shape structured, coherent episodic memory formation for curiosity-driven active learning agents by rewarding reward signal along each incremental step of memory encoding and recall rather than only scoring final task outcomes. This creates a self-organizing memory hierarchy where only useful, contextually consistent memory traces are retained and refined over the agent's active learning lifespan.
